title: excoecaria glaucescens
intro: Excoecaria glaucescens, also known as the poison tree, is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Australia and Asia. The plant contains a toxic compound called phorbol ester, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria glaucescens include reducing inflammation and pain, treating wounds, and potentially preventing the growth of certain types of bacteria. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make topical ointments or creams, which are applied directly to the affected area to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
